# Conroe ISD Board Meeting — 2021-12-14
*Regular Meeting — Unofficial Notes*
*Based on auto-generated transcript. Not an official record.*
*Generated 2026-03-28 14:11 UTC*

---

# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees
### Regular Meeting — December 14, 2021

> **Note:** These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript and may contain errors or omissions. Please consult official board minutes for the authoritative record.

---

## Opening
- Meeting called to order at **6:00 p.m.** with a quorum present.
- **Invocation** by Scott Moore, who offered a prayer acknowledging the holiday season and asking for guidance in the board's decisions.
- **Pledge of Allegiance** led by Stacy Chase (U.S. and Texas pledges).

---

## Superintendent's Holiday Message (Dr. Noel)
- Wished everyone a Merry Christmas and happy holidays.
- Shared a heartwarming story about CISD Police Officer **Jonathan Guerra** at Milam Elementary, who purchased a book for a student at the Scholastic Book Fair when the child didn't have enough money, and told the boy to keep his remaining money to take home to his mother.
- Highlighted the **Children and Santa's Dreams Toy Drive**, noting that over **600 families** were impacted and **1,500 children** will receive Christmas gifts thanks to CISD police and community contributions.

---

## Item 2A: Special Board Recognition — Destination Imagination Global Champions

Presented by **Chris Reichelt**. Conroe ISD had **53 teams** participate in Destination Imagination during 2020–2021; **three teams** won first place at the virtual DI Global Competition:

1. **The Timekeepers** — Academy of Science and Technology
   - Won 1st place (secondary level Fine Arts challenge) for the **second year in a row**
   - Also won the **Renaissance Award** for exceptional creative artistry, workmanship, and storytelling
   - Coaches: Cindy Kim and John Anderson White
   - Members: Abram Allen, Neyman Aurora, Marley Bartholomew, Jonathan Kim, Rihanna Patel

2. **The D.I. Janes** — The Woodlands College Park High School
   - Won 1st place (secondary level Engineering challenge)
   - Coaches: Rochelle Socon and Kelly Jones
   - Members: Emily Jonic, Nanette Socon, Michaela Barnes

3. **The Chicks with Kicks** — Tom Cox Intermediate
   - Won 1st place (middle level Technical Fast Flex challenge)
   - Coaches: Marilyn Weisenhunt and Stephanie Hilbridge
   - Members: Marie Weisenhunt, Riley Brashers, Natalie Hilbridge, Abigail Sorosick

- Reichelt personally thanked the board for continued support of the DI program, noting it gives students who may not perform on athletic fields a chance to shine academically.
- Board member **Mr. Williams** congratulated the students, calling them "some of the best and brightest of CISD" and encouraging their entrepreneurial and innovative spirit.

---

## Item 2B: Special Board Recognition — 2021 Lone Star State School Counselor of the Year

Presented by **Denis Sapola** (Coordinator) and **Tiffany Rodriguez** (Caney Creek HS Lead Counselor / LSCA President).

- **Caney Creek High School** received the **Lone Star State School Counselor Association (LSCA) Gold Award** for 2021 and is now in consideration for the national **ASCA RAMP Award**.

- **Ashley Wright**, School Counselor at **Lucille J. Bradley Elementary School**, was named:
  - **2021 Texas School Counselor of the Year** by LSCA
  - One of the **top five national finalists** for the **ASCA 2022 School Counselor of the Year** (selected from all 50+ state winners)
  - She will be honored at an in-person celebration in **Washington, D.C. on February 3, 2022**.

- Ms. Wright addressed the board, expressing gratitude and passion for school counseling, advocating for continued investment in guidance and counseling departments and programs.

---

## Item 3: Citizen Participation

### Speaker 1: Diane Daniels
- Wished the board Merry Christmas and Happy New Year.
- Acknowledged the positive work of CISD teachers and testing achievements.
- Expressed concern about the **$64 million in ESSA funding**, stating "nothing comes free" and there are likely strings attached.
- Suggested the district consider **returning the money** if the conditions attached conflict with what parents want.
- Asked the board to listen to parents and be transparent about how the funding will be used.

### Speaker 2: Anthony Natuno
- Returned to discuss an issue he raised over a year ago about **classroom composition and student accommodations**.
- Argued that the **22:1 student-teacher ratio** (the "bright line test") does not account for the number and type of accommodations (IEPs, 504 plans) teachers must provide in individual classrooms.
- Used a visual aid to show how some classrooms have 4–5 students requiring accommodations while adjacent classrooms have fewer, creating unequal stress on teachers and risk to students.
- Stated the district is **still not monitoring classroom accommodation loads** and urged the board to consider this in student placement.

### Speaker 3: Lorena Perez-McGill
- Parent of a 5th grader at Wilkerson Intermediate and a junior at College Park High School.
- Thanked board members and administrators who understand their roles are to **serve all students**.
- Referenced the previous month's meeting where speakers expressed displeasure about perceived indoctrination and expressed negative views about homosexuality and transgenderism.
- Thanked the board for serving all students **regardless of race, creed, gender, or sexual orientation**.
- Wished happy holidays and Merry Christmas.

---

## Item 4: Consent Agenda
- No items were pulled for separate discussion.
- **Motion:** To approve the consent agenda as presented.
- **Vote: Approved unanimously.**

---

## Item 5A: Planning & Construction — Guaranteed Maximum Price for Campus Renovations 2022

Presented by **Mr. Foster**.

- Annual project replacing building systems that have reached end of service life (described as an "invasive maintenance program").
- **GTT General Contractors** serves as construction manager at risk (selected February 23, 2021; has served in this role for several years).
- Design architect: **DLR Group**.
- **Guaranteed Maximum Price: $5,689,000**
- **Motion:** To approve.
- **Vote: Approved unanimously.**

---

## Item 5B: Capital Improvement Updates (Informational)

Mr. Foster provided updates on multiple projects:

| Project | Status | Key Details |
|---|---|---|
| **Creighton Elementary** (overhaul) | On schedule; completion Summer 2022 | Working on exterior masonry for kitchen/serving line expansion; major kitchen remodel planned for end of school year |
| **Moorhead Junior High** (new campus, adjacent to Caney Creek HS) | On schedule; opening August 2023 | Structural steel on site; bar joists being installed; building systems progressing through fine arts, CTE, and academic wings |
| **Conroe High School** (major renovation/reorganization) | On schedule; completion through 2025 | CTE building slab ready to go vertical; athletic venue foundations progressing; new football stadium for JV/soccer underway; structural steel for CTE arriving later in December |
| **New Jet Center** (north end zone, Woodforest Bank Stadium) | Completion February 2022; district use in spring semester | Windows being installed; interior finishing (ceiling grid, light fixtures) nearing completion |
| **Gordon Reed Elementary** (new campus, Conroe HS feeder zone, west side) | On schedule; opening August 2022 | Exterior masonry progressing; interior painting with spirit colors underway |
| **Oak Ridge High School** (overhaul + south county CTE campus) | On schedule; through August 2023 | Underground utility work; major fiber connection completed over recent weekend; foundation and vertical progress expected soon |
| **Wilkerson Intermediate** (PE classroom addition) | Working toward January opening | Supply chain challenges; contractor on 7-day work schedule; gym expected for PE use in spring semester; kitchen renovations and final connections to follow in spring |
| **Conroe High School 9th Grade Classroom Addition** | On schedule; opening August 2022 | Exterior masonry nearing completion; roof cap sheet next; interior walls and building systems progressing; exterior courtyard for student dining turned over mid-project for spring use |

- **Notable comment:** Dr. Noel noted that UIL membership numbers show **Conroe High School is now the 4th largest high school in Texas**, growing by approximately **200 students per year**, underscoring the need for these additions. He thanked voters who passed the bond.

---

## Item 6A: Business & Purchasing — CSP 21-1003 Internal Connections, Gordon Reed Elementary (E-Rate)

Presented by **Mr. Streeps**.

- Award of internal connections technology project for new Gordon Reed Elementary to **Datavox Inc.**
- **Estimated expenditure: $550,000**
- E-Rate project (federal program); Form 470 filed with USAC; advertised on CIC purchasing website and twice in the Conroe Courier.
- Scope includes: wireless access points, routers, switches, racks, phones, UPS, cabling and components.
- Three qualifying bids received; evaluated by technology department and reviewed by purchasing.
- Pricing effective through **September 30, 2023**; work may begin **April 1, 2022**.
- Funding from **Capital Projects Fund**.
- **Motion:** To approve as presented.
- **Vote: Approved unanimously.**

---

## Item 7A: Financial Reports

Presented by **Ms. Garza**.

### General Fund (as of November 30):
- Total assets: **$603.8 million**
- Year-to-date revenue: **$130.8 million**
- Year-to-date expenses: **$94.9 million**

### Debt Service Fund:
- Total assets: **$132.6 million**

### Child Nutrition:
- Total assets: **$6.8 million**

### Tax Collections:
- Currently at **8.1%** of the **$507 million** levy (first update of fiscal year; bulk expected in December and January).

### Self-Funded Insurance:
- November: Revenue $4.5M, Expenses $5.8M — **deficit of $1.3 million** for the month.
- Believed to be related to **elective surgeries postponed during COVID** now being completed.
- Fund balance: **$7.3 million**
- Insurance consultant **Gallagher** reviewing claims to verify the cause.
- **Board discussion:** A board member asked about sustainability; Ms. Garza noted that when the plan was set, the board intentionally kept rates low and included a **5% COVID adjustment**, choosing to absorb the hit in the fund balance rather than pass costs to employees. December was trending toward a surplus. The expected COVID-related overage for the year was projected at **$2.5–3 million**.

### Investments:
- Par value: **$441.6 million**
- Pool yield: **0.073%**
- Longer-term investments (TCG): weighted average maturity 583 days, yielding **0.828%**
- Combined portfolio: **0.186%**
- Benchmark (90-day T-bill): **0.045%**

### Wellness Centers:
- Averaging **358 visits** for the year.

---

## Executive Session
- Board entered **closed session at 6:53 p.m.** under Texas Government Code Sections 551.071 (legal), 551.072 (real property), and 551.074 (personnel).
- Board returned to **open session at 7:17 p.m.**

---

## Item 10: Legal — Purchase of Property in Caney Creek HS Feeder Zone
- Board considered purchase of **approximately 1.46 acres** in the Caney Creek High School feeder zone.
- Contract nearly finalized; board asked to approve the purchase and **delegate final signing authority and negotiations to Dr. Noel**.
- **Motion:** To approve.
- **Vote: Approved unanimously.**

---

## Adjournment
- Meeting adjourned at approximately **7:17+ p.m.**

---

## Summary of All Votes

| Item | Description | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Consent Agenda | Approval as presented | **Unanimous** |
| GMP — Campus Renovations 2022 ($5,689,000) | Approve and authorize superintendent | **Unanimous** |
| CSP 21-1003 — Gordon Reed Elementary E-Rate ($550,000) | Award to Datavox Inc. | **Unanimous** |
| Property Purchase — ~1.46 acres, Caney Creek feeder zone | Approve and delegate authority to Dr. Noel | **Unanimous** |
